title: Proteomics workflow for APP/Aβ TOMAHAQ analysis in endosomal and lysosomal fractions
author: Hankum Park
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bys8pwhw
materials: For the absolute quantification of the target peptides, apply selected ion monitoring (SIM) experiments. Perform SIM experiments on a Thermo Scientific Orbitrap Eclipse Tribrid mass spectrometer coupled to an Easy-nLC 1200 UHPLC system. Load each sample on an in-house packed C18 column (30 cm, 2.6 um Accucore [Thermo Fisher], 100 µm I.D.), and elute using a 150-min method over a gradient from 5% to 38% B (95% acetonitrile, 0.125% formic acid). Monitor target peptides within a 50 min window around the scheduled retention time. Isolate a pair of trigger and target peptides, and accumulated separately targeting same AGC value (5*10 4 ; resolution at 240,000) and subsequently analyze in a single Orbitrap SIM scan. If two target peptides share similar retention time and same FAIMS CV values, multiplex their detections into a single SIM scan.

steps.title: Prepare all samples (unfiltered PNS, Lyso,...
steps.instruction: Prepare all samples (unfiltered PNS, Lyso, and Endo; Amicon-filtered PNS_LMW, Lyso_LMW, and Endo_LMW) as described (dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.byjfpujn). For full peptide sequences and associated proteomic parameters, see the attached document.

steps.title: Reduce all samples by adding TCEP...
steps.instruction: Reduce all samples by adding TCEP to 5 mM final and incubate at 25 ºC for 30 min with shaking.

steps.title: Alkylate cysteines by adding iodoacetamide to15...
steps.instruction: Alkylate cysteines by adding iodoacetamide to15 mM final and incubate at 25 ºC for 30 min with shaking and protected from light.

steps.title: Dilute samples with EPPS buffer for 1
steps.instruction: Dilute samples with EPPS buffer for 1.2 M urea final concentration.

steps.title: Add 6
steps.instruction: Add 6.1 N TCA solution to 20% final and incubate at 4 ºC for 1.5h. Centrifuge samples at 21,000xg for 15 min at 4 ºC and remove supernatants.

steps.title: Wash twice with ice-cold acetone by...
steps.instruction: Wash twice with ice-cold acetone by centrifuging at 21,000xg for 10 min at 4 ºC. After final wash, briefly dry protein pellets in a SpeedVac.

steps.title: Resuspend pellets in 10 µL of...
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellets in 10 µL of 8 M urea buffer, sonicate in a water bath sonicator, and dilute urea by adding 10 µL of 200 mM EPPS.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.3 µg of LysC and incubate at 37 ºC for 2h with shaking. Further dilute urea to 1.6 M final by adding 200 mM EPPS.

steps.title: Further digest peptides by adding 0
steps.instruction: Further digest peptides by adding 0.4 µg trypsin and incubate at 37 ºC overnight with shaking.

steps.title: The next day, add acetonitrile (ACN)...
steps.instruction: The next day, add acetonitrile (ACN) to 30% final, and label peptides by adding 3.6-5.3 µL of TMT 11-plex reagents (10 μg/μL in anhydrous ACN) for 1h at 25 ºC with shaking. Quench labeling reaction by adding hydroxylamine to 0.5% final followed by incubation at room temperature for 15 min.

steps.title: Dry pooled sample by SpeedVac, and desalt by C18 StageTip
steps.instruction: Dry pooled sample by SpeedVac, and desalt by C18 StageTip.

steps.title: Add synthetic reference trigger peptides labeled...
steps.instruction: Add synthetic reference trigger peptides labeled with super-heavy TMTsh as described ( dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.byjcpuiw ). The peptides employed and their associated ionization and fragmentation properties are provided in the attached spreadsheet.

steps.title: Perform TOMAHAQ experiments using the Tomahto...
steps.instruction: Perform TOMAHAQ experiments using the Tomahto software package on a Thermo Scientific Orbitrap Eclipse Tribrid mass spectrometer coupled to an Easy-nLC 1200 UHPLC system. Load each sample on a C18 column (30 cm, 2.6 μm Accucore [Thermo Fisher], 100 µm I.D.), and eluted using a 150-min method over a gradient from 5% to 38% B (95% ACN/0.125% formic acid). The instrument method only controlls Orbitrap MS 1 scans (resolution at 120,000; mass range 300−1500 m/z; automatic gain control (AGC) target 2*10 5 , maximum injection time 50 ms). Peptide targets are imported into Tomahto, and the following decisions are made by Tomahto in real-time.

steps.title: Export summed signal-to-noise ratio (S/N) to...
steps.instruction: Export summed signal-to-noise ratio (S/N) to csv file, and analyze by R 3.6.3. Adjust S/N of each channel using the isotopic impurity table of TMT reagents provided by the vendor. Normalize the adjusted S/N values according to the total reporter values in each channel according to a SPS-MS 3 analysis, assuming equal amount of loading. Test a statistical significance between DMSO- and compound-treated group using two-sided Student’s t -test with t_test() function in the rstatix package version 0.7.0.

steps.title: Import RAW files from the SIM...
steps.instruction: Import RAW files from the SIM experiments into Skyline, and extract the precursor ion peaks with 10 ppm accuracy. Because the TMT-labeled target peptides and TMTsh-labeled trigger peptides have the same retention time, measure the area under each peak, and calculate the ratio between target and trigger. Derive absolute amount the target peptide by multiplying the ratio by the known amount of the trigger peptide. Divide the absolute amount of target peptide by the relative quantitation from TOMAHAQ to calculate the absolute amount from each channel.
